Fallback and Kernel Dispatch:

GPUs like the NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti and AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT have a built-in fallback mechanism that allows them to run unsupported or unoptimized code. This fallback is typically implemented using a kernel dispatch, which is a set of instructions that are executed when the GPU cannot execute the requested operation.

Kernel Dispatch:

A kernel dispatch is a set of instructions that are executed by the GPU when it cannot execute the requested operation. The kernel dispatch is typically implemented using a combination of hardware and software techniques to optimize the performance of the GPU.
